content.full_text:
CITY OF FAIRFIELD
1000 WEBSTER STREET
FAIRFIELD, CA 94533
[707] 428-7419

MAR 29 2:59 PM '90
Incorporated December 12, 1903

March 28, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Conflict of Interest Division
428 J Street - Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

re: Request for Advice

Dear Sir or Madam:

I am the City Attorney of the City of Fairfield and I am writing this request for advice on behalf of Jay Bodutch, an Assistant Director of Planning and Development for the City of Fairfield.

I am familiar with FPPC Advice Letter No. I-89-575 (December 26, 1989 to Don Vickers, Deputy County Counsel, Santa Barbara) in which you state that membership in a country club is an asset which may require disqualification if the membership is transferable for value. The purpose of this request for advice is to determine whether the opinion in Advice Letter No. I-89-575 applies to the facts involving Mr. Bodutch.

1. Mr. Bodutch is a member of the Rancho Solano Resort and Racquet Club, a private, for profit club located in the City of Fairfield. Mr. Bodutch paid a membership fee to join the club and the membership is transferable for value.

2. The Rancho Solano Resort and Racquet Club is located adjacent to the City-owned Rancho Solano Golf Course. The Club consists of tennis courts, racquet ball courts, and exercise facilities. Club members have access to the golf course on the same terms as members of the public who are not club members.

3. In addition to the golf course and private club facilities, there is a clubhouse building which houses a fine dining restaurant, a bar and grill facility, a pro shop, and meeting rooms. The clubhouse is owned by the City of Fairfield and leased to the Rancho Solano Resort and Racquet Club, a California general partnership. The clubhouse facilities are open to all members of the public.

4. A second City-owned golf course is proposed in Paradise Valley, in the northeastern section of the City of Fairfield. The Paradise Valley Golf Course will not include a private club or clubhouse facilities.

5.    Mr. Bodutch's membership in the Rancho Solano Resort and
Racquet Club is "financial interest" as that term is used in
Government Code §87103.

6.    Mr. Bodutch may not make, participate in making, or use his
position to influence a decision which will increase or decrease
the value of the memberships by at least $250.

7.    As an Assistant Director of Planning and Development, Mr.
Bodutch has been assigned to coordinate the development of the
Paradise Valley Golf Course.

Is Mr. Bodutch prohibited from coordinating the development of
the Paradise Valley Golf Course because that project may have a
material financial effect on his Rancho Solano Resort and Racquet
Club membership?


Thank you for your attention.  Please feel free to call me if you
have questions or need further information.


Sincerely,

[signature]
Betsy Strauss
City Attorney

c:  Jay Bodutch
    Bill Daugherty

sections.question: Is Mr. Bodutch prohibited from coordinating the development of the Paradise Valley Golf Course because that project may have a material financial effect on his Rancho Solano Resort and Racquet Club membership?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an Assistant Director of Planning must disqualify himself from coordinating development of a competing municipal golf course when he holds a transferable membership in a private club adjacent to an existing city golf course.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request to FPPC, not an FPPC response. References prior FPPC Advice Letter No. I-89-575. Contains numbered facts/statements (items 1-7) establishing the situation, followed by the specific legal question. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section present.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Is Mr. Bodutch prohibited from coordinating the development of the Paradise Valley Golf Course because that project may have a material financial effect on his Rancho Solano Resort and Racquet Club membership?

embedding.summary: City Attorney of Fairfield requests FPPC advice on whether Assistant Director of Planning Jay Bodutch must disqualify himself from coordinating development of a proposed Paradise Valley Golf Course due to his financial interest in a membership at the adjacent Rancho Solano Resort and Racquet Club.
